# Transcrição: The_Platform_Owes_You_Nothing

- **Fonte:** The_Platform_Owes_You_Nothing
- **Duração:** 01:29
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:29]

The platform owes you nothing. Consider this a warning if you're an online creator who relies on your content and audiences you built with it to drive revenue. Do not take it for granted. Do not assume what works today will work tomorrow. Newsfeed algorithms change. Platforms implode. Add costs, competition, and effectiveness change. User consumption evolves from one format to another. Email regulation impacts deliverability and effectiveness. Search engines change what they favor and evolve to focus on AI chat conversations. If you assume you can keep doing what you're doing and be able to easily pivot if you need to, you're in for a rude awakening. I could say this because that was me. I built something amazing pretty darn quickly. I felt special. I leaned into the Facebook platform, blogging, traffic that Google sent me, and the email list that resulted from it. It was far more successful than anything I could have dreamed of. I took it for granted. I assumed it would keep working. And if it stopped working, I'd be able to easily pivot to something else and leverage that first thing that I built. It didn't work that way. I made my pivot one year ago, but it may have been too late. It's possible that you can't see this now. You're a well-oiled machine. A combination of hard work, strategy, good timing, and good fortune help you get here. But it doesn't guarantee it'll get your business to tomorrow. The platforms and formats you built your business on owe you nothing. Consistently evolve. Be prepared to make difficult changes. And be curious of that new thing rather than resistant to it.
